evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: fast-moving environment; Balance speed and quality; high-stakes; intense; relentless drive to win; move fast
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Velocity dominates: repeated emphasis on speed (fast-moving, balance speed/quality), intensity, and fast execution with minimal process.
evidence.domain_risk: AI-powered insurance; financial safety products; SME insurance
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Insurtech domain but KPIs focus on product adoption and experimentation, not regulatory/compliance—moderately risky.
evidence.collaboration_width: Product; Insurance departments; colleagues across functions
evidence.data_team_maturity: first data hire in a sense (analytics engineer role as primary execution); help build data infrastructure
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Early stage: role involves building dashboards and data pipelines from scratch in a high-growth context without established data team.
evidence.jd_authorship: Create and maintain interactive dashboards (using Databricks and QuickSight); Contribute to the data transformation code base (using dbt and Git); A/B tests
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific tools (Databricks, QuickSight, dbt, Git) and methodologies (A/B testing, KPI definition) show hiring manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Product and feature launches; A/B tests; experimentation; product roadmap
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary stakeholder is Product and experimentation teams; focus is on product-driven metrics and feature adoption.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own outcomes within your scope — from problem definition to delivery; Operate in ambiguity; Balance speed and quality
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Execution framing: 'own outcomes' and work independently but within defined scopes, not strategic direction-setting.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools expected from candidate; company uses AI but role focuses on data analytics, not AI-enabled work.
evidence.testing_framing: A/B testing; statistics and A/B testing; analyse A/B tests
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: A/B testing mentioned in requirements/skills list but not as candidate's named ownership responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: ensure we draw the right conclusions from every experiment; integrity; intellectual honesty
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: experimental rigor and decision integrity are concerns but not framed as compliance/risk control.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Create and maintain; build data infrastructure; Contribute to the data transformation code base
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Create and build verbs suggest some greenfield work in early-stage data function.
